# 🌸 Word Devs — Contributing Guidelines

<div align="center">

<img src="../Src/Assets/Images/Banners/contrib-banner.png" alt="Word Devs Contributing Guidelines" width="100%">

    ٩(◕‿◕｡)۶

**We are so excited to have you here!**
_Thank you for wanting to make Word Devs even more awesome_ 💜✨

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

</div>

First off, thank you for taking the time to contribute! Whether you are fixing a tiny typo in our docs, squashing a nasty bug, or implementing an entire new feature, your help makes a huge difference. 🌸

Please take a moment to read through these guidelines to make the contribution process as smooth and happy as possible for everyone.

---

## 🗺️ How Can I Contribute?

### 🪲 1. Reporting Bugs & Suggesting Features

If you found something broken or have a brilliant new idea:

- Check if there's already an open issue in our [Issue Tracker](../issues) to avoid duplicates.
- If not, open a new issue using our cute templates! We have dedicated forms for [Bug Reports](../issues/new?template=bug_report.md) and [Feature Requests](../issues/new?template=feature_request.md).

### 🛠️ 2. Submitting a Pull Request (PR)

Ready to write some code? Yay! Here is our standard workflow:

1.  **Fork** the repository to your own GitHub account.
2.  **Clone** your fork locally:
    ```bash
    git clone [https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/repository-name.git](https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/repository-name.git)
    ```
3.  **Create a new branch** for your changes. Keep the name short and descriptive:
    ```bash
    git checkout -b feat/your-awesome-feature
    # or
    git checkout -b fix/annoying-bug-name
    ```
4.  **Write your code!** Make sure your editor respects our `.editorconfig` rules (2 spaces indent, UTF-8, etc.) to keep the styling perfectly consistent.
5.  **Commit your changes** using clean, descriptive commit messages:
    ```bash
    git commit -m "feat: add beautiful new card generator layout"
    ```
6.  **Push** your branch to your fork:
    ```bash
    git push origin feat/your-awesome-feature
    ```
7.  Open a **Pull Request** against our `main` or `master` branch.

---

## 📋 Pull Request Checklist

Before submitting your PR, please make sure you've ticked these boxes:

- [ ] **Alignment:** Your changes match the scope and spirit of the project.
- [ ] **Checklist:** You filled out our [Pull Request Template](../PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md) completely.
- [ ] **Tests:** If the repository has automated tests, make sure they all pass!
- [ ] **Linting:** Run the linter to ensure your Markdown or JS files are perfectly formatted.
- [ ] **Documentation:** If you added a new feature, don't forget to update the README or relevant documentation!

---

## 🌸 Style & Code Guidelines

- **Be Consistent:** Try to match the existing coding style of the repository you are working on.
- **Keep it Clean:** Avoid leaving commented-out debug code, console logs (unless necessary), or unused dependencies.
- **Keep it Friendly:** Write code comments that are helpful and friendly for other developers who will read them later.

---

## 🛡️ Respecting Our Community

By contributing to Word Devs, you agree to uphold and respect our [Code of Conduct](../blob/main/.github/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) at all times. Let's keep this space kind, supportive, and completely free of harassment!
